questions.question: 3 (a) A scientist determines the density of a sample of sea water. Describe an experiment to determine the density of the sea water. You may draw a diagram as part of your answer. Include any equations in your answer. ................................................................................................................................................... ..........................
questions.text: 3 (a) A scientist determines the density of a sample of sea water. Describe an experiment to determine the density of the sea water. You may draw a diagram as part of your answer. Include any equations in your answer. ................................................................................................................................................... ..........................

parts.answer: liquid does a plastic object float? does a wooden object float? sea water yes yes baby oil no yes B1 B1 October/November 2025 Question Answer Marks B1 any three from the following: • liquid (particles) → gas / vapour (particles) • happens at a surface • (liquid / water absorbs) thermal energy / heat from sun / surroundings / remaining liquid • (average) kinetic energy of particle increases • more energetic particles escape owtte B3 B1 October/November 2025 Question Answer Marks

questions.question: 5 (a) Table 5.1 shows whether some energy resources are renewable and whether they cause air pollution when being used to generate electrical power. For each energy resource, complete Table 5.1 by writing yes or no in each space. The solar energy resource has been done for you. Table 5.1 energy resourceis it renewable?does it cause air pollution? solar yesno fossil fuels wind nuclear fuel tida

parts.answer: energy resource is it renewable? does it cause air pollution? solar yes no fossil fuels no yes wind yes no nuclear fuel no no tidal yes no B1 B1 B1 B1 5(.0)  10 –7 (m) A3 3.0 ( 10 ) ÷ 6.0 ( 10 ) C2 (wavelength =) speed ÷ frequency C1 matching use B1 October/November 2025 Question Answer Marks ray continued in a straight line to cross drawn ray M1 or ray from top of object through principal focus on LHS of lens to the centre of the lens ray continued as a straight paraxial line to cross drawn ray A1 point where rays cross indicated C1 October/November 2025 Question Answer Marks one or two (from cell(s) / battery, lamp and switch) circuit symbols correct C1 a complete circuit with cell (s), lamp and switch in series B1 24 () A3 6(.0) ÷ 0.25 C2 (resistance =) potential difference ÷ current C1 6(.0)  0.25 C2 (power =) potential difference  current C1 October/November 2025 Question Answer Marks • smaller current (in cables) • more efficient • less power / heating losses • thinner / cheaper cables / pylons further apart owtte • less voltage drop • allows for transmission over a longer distance • fewer / larger power stations can supply more area / whole country B2 of thermal radiation / infrared A1 • two coils • copper wire / coils • (coils wrapped round / linked by soft) iron core • more turns on primary coil B3 October/November 2025 Question Answer Marks

parts.answer: P –  / beta (particle) B1 Q –  / alpha (particle) B1 R –  / gamma (ray) B1 • use a lead (lined) box / container or concrete room • idea of remote / area location / away from main building • locked room / secure area • radiation sign on door / in place • only remove / use for short time owtte B2 proton electron neutral / zero / 0 inside the nucleus outside the nucleus October/November 2025 Question Answer Marks
